articles.title: Oil Climbs as US-Iran Deadlock Lifts Bond Yields

A renewed advance in oil prices sent bonds lower after the US and Iran failed to agree on terms to end their war, dashing hopes for a revival of the Strait of Hormuz while stoking inflation concerns. The Treasury market, which has priced out the odds of Federal Reserve rate cuts this year amid the Middle East conflict, saw an increase in yields. articles.title: Israeli strikes hit south Lebanon, killing three and wounding four
articles.summary: Israeli strikes hit south Lebanon, killing three and wounding four Israeli forces have carried out multiple strikes across southern Lebanon, killing at least three people and injuring four others, according to Lebanon’s National News Agency. In the town of Srifa, Israeli forces targeted a health facility, killing one person and wounding four, in what officials described as a direct strike on a clinic. In a separate attack, an Israeli drone fired a guided missile at a car in Doueir, killing two people. The strike hit a vehicle on a local road earlier in the day.

articles.summary: Aramco: Current energy supply shock 'largest ever experienced' Aramco's CEO Amin Nasser said the current energy supply shock which hit during its first quarter is the "largest the world has ever experienced.” He added that even if the Strait of Hormuz were to open today, it would still require months for markets to restore balance, and that if the Hormuz “disruption” were to carry on for a few more weeks, market normalisation wouldn't happen until 2027.
